narrative:
On June 20, 2026, the Karnataka High Court registered a 100% overall hearing rate, resolving all 2 scheduled cases. This represents a significant contraction in docket volume compared to preceding weekdays, such as June 19, when the court scheduled 2,256 cases and heard 1,125 (49.9%). The caseload was similarly high earlier in the week, with hearing rates of 44.0% on June 18 (1,142 heard of 2,596) and 43.5% on June 17 (1,157 heard of 2,661).

The entire docket for June 20 was handled in Court Hall 9 by Justice Ashok S. Kinagi, who achieved a perfect 100% hearing rate for both scheduled matters. 

In terms of hearing stages and case types, both scheduled matters were Writ Petitions (WP) listed under the Preliminary Hearing stage, yielding a 100% conversion rate. This focused clearance stands in contrast to the typical weekday distribution. For instance, on June 19, Writ Petitions represented the largest case category but recorded a much lower hearing rate of 45.1% (410 of 910 cases heard), while the dominant stages were Orders (53.7% heard) and Admissions (46.4% heard). The specialized, low-volume session on June 20 allowed for complete disposition of its scheduled administrative and preliminary writ matters.
